response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, craftsmanship-oriented full-stack engineer with genuine strengths in testing culture and community teaching, but the profile diverges sharply from this role's core requirements. C++ experience is limited to a ~7-month stint at National Instruments and a thesis project, far short of the required 8 years, and most infrastructure exposure is application-level (Node.js, TypeScript, DLang) rather than building large-scale cloud compute platforms. Leadership evidence is informal (workshops, dojos, a founder side-project) rather than the sustained technical-lead experience mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers that this position demands.
strengths: Hands-on high-throughput, low-latency systems work at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP requests/sec, async I/O, caching)
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and authored testing tooling
Proven mentoring inclination via workshops, courses, and refactoring dojos
concerns: Nowhere near the required 8 years of C++ programming experience
No demonstrated ownership of large-scale cloud compute or infrastructure platform architecture
No formal technical leadership role leading strategy, priorities, and cross-team alignment

key_factors.factor: Lack of cloud platform / infrastructure leadership
